news.title: Goa: GIDC allots three plots for ambitious IT park project
news.para: Goa's Verna industrial estate is set to host the 'Hello World Innovation & Research Park,' with three of its six large plots already allocated to a Mumbai-based developer. This ambitious project, expected to create 20,000-30,000 jobs, aims to attract skilled professionals back to the state.  The developer has sought clarity on infrastructure timelines, raising concerns about potential delays impacting project commencement.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government to set up 500 GCC units by 2031: CM
news.para: Uttar Pradesh is positioning itself as India's next major hub for Global Capability Centres and technology investments. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath invited global firms to "build the future in Uttar Pradesh," highlighting a skilled workforce, improved infrastructure, and investor-friendly policies. The state aims for 40 million sq ft of office space and 500 GCC units by 2031, securing over Rs 50,000 crore in investment commitments during a recent dialogue.

news.title: Tenants told to shift from illegal PGs and guesthouses by June 30 in Gurugram
news.para: Gurugram residents in illegal paying guest accommodations, guesthouses, and commercial establishments operating from residential plots in DLF Phases 1-5 have been urged to find new housing by June 30. The Town and Country Planning department is cracking down on violations of zoning and building norms. Many occupants were unaware of prior notices to property owners, prompting this public advisory to prevent inconvenience from sealing actions.

news.title: Hyderabad development body's Medipally auction nets ₹120 crore amid strong demand
news.para: Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority's recent land auction in Medipally proved a resounding success, raking in nearly ₹120 crore from 68 plots. Bids significantly surpassed the reserve price, with one plot fetching ₹80,000 per square yard. This strong market response, despite a court stay on another sale, highlights robust confidence in HMDA-developed layouts and growing demand for planned residential spaces across the region.

news.title: Vedanta announces foray into real estate sector
news.para: Vedanta Ltd has launched a new wholly-owned subsidiary, Vedanta Property Platforms Ltd (VPPL), to enter the real estate sector. This strategic move aims to unlock value from surplus land and non-core assets, potentially funding expansion in its core metals and energy businesses. VPPL, incorporated in Mumbai, will act as a dedicated platform for real estate ventures and asset-light initiatives.

news.title: NCR plans four new cities along RRTS corridors to ease housing costs
news.para: The National Capital Region Planning Board’s Regional Plan 2041 proposes four new greenfield cities along Rapid Rail Transit System (RRTS) corridors, a move experts say could improve affordability and reshape real estate growth across NCR.

news.title: Prologis takes $16.6 billion bid for UK's Segro public after rejection
news.para: US logistics giant Prologis has publicly revealed a £12.6 billion takeover bid for British firm Segro after its initial approach was rejected. Prologis aims to pressure Segro's board to negotiate, citing the UK's attractive valuations for foreign buyers. Segro's shares jumped on the news, but the company has unequivocally dismissed the offer as opportunistic and falling short of its value.

news.title: US new single-family home sales post second straight monthly decline
news.para: US new home sales unexpectedly dropped in May, a second consecutive monthly decline, as higher mortgage rates and prices deter buyers. Despite a recent bill aimed at affordability, experts say more action is needed to address the housing shortage and unaffordable prices. Builders are facing a growing inventory overhang, pushing back hopes for a housing market recovery to potentially 2027.

news.title: West Bengal CM suspends construction of commercial projects in Kolkata till July 31
news.para: Following a fatal warehouse collapse, West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has halted all under-construction commercial projects approved by the previous government until July 31. A comprehensive audit will scrutinize these projects, including those built on filled water bodies, to ensure adherence to safety norms. Essential services like hospitals and fire stations are exempt.

news.title: Uttarakhand CM mandates statewide building safety audits
news.para: Following a tragic fire in Lucknow that claimed 15 lives, Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami has ordered comprehensive safety audits of all large buildings in the state.  Meanwhile, Lucknow authorities sealed a coaching centre due to multiple safety violations and improper building use. The building owner has been remanded to judicial custody. Inspections of other coaching centres are underway.

news.title: Embassy Developments signs non-binding MoU with Uttar Pradesh govt for ₹1,500 crore commercial project
news.para: Embassy Developments is set to make its debut in Uttar Pradesh's commercial real estate scene with a significant project in Lucknow. A non-binding MoU with the state government outlines a plan for a 2.5-3 million sq ft office-led development, backed by an investment of approximately ₹1,500 crore. This initiative aims to bolster business infrastructure and generate employment opportunities within the region.

news.title: ORA Group forays into integrated township and plotted development through ORA Land; Unveils vision for landmark community at Karjat
news.para: ORA Group is expanding into integrated townships and plotted developments with ORA Land, launching its first project, 'BluBay,' in Karjat. This move aims to redefine land ownership with modern infrastructure and lifestyle amenities. Indian cricketer Suryakumar Yadav has been appointed as the brand ambassador for BluBay, highlighting a vision for balanced and meaningful living. The development capitalizes on Karjat's growing appeal as a real estate destination.

news.title: Indore civic body issues notices to 47 over building permit fraud
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation has issued notices to 47 property owners and consultants for manipulating building permissions to avoid fees and reduce taxes.  Scrutiny of online approvals revealed applicants exploited the automated system, falsely claiming vacant land, amalgamating plots, and under-reporting construction areas. This allowed them to pay significantly less than mandated fees. The civic body is now reviewing past permits to recover dues and penalize offenders.

news.title: Telangana high court defers hearing; Hakimpet auction on hold
news.para: Telangana High Court has halted the e-auction of an 8.24-acre land parcel in Hakimpet, Banjara Hills, after petitioners claimed ownership. The court appointed two advocate commissioners to inspect the disputed land and submit a report. The petitioners argue that boundary disputes with adjoining lands remain unresolved, and the auction proceeded without a joint survey. The next hearing is scheduled for July 20.

news.title: Official suspended over illegal A-khatas to 50 properties in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru West City Corporation has suspended assistant revenue officer K.S. Manjula for allegedly issuing A-khatas to over 50 properties in an unauthorized layout. The land, located in Herohalli village, was under litigation with a court order to maintain status quo. Officials cite serious tampering with records and violation of service rules, initiating a departmental inquiry.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh mandated three-year electrical safety checks for commercial buildings in 2023
news.para: Despite a 2023 directive mandating periodic electrical safety checks for commercial buildings following a fatal hotel fire, a recent tragedy in Aliganj highlights a stark failure in enforcement. The Uttar Pradesh government had ordered inspections every three years and required No Objection Certificates (NOCs) from the Directorate of Electrical Safety.

news.title: Nagpur: 103 buildings housing coaching classes are unsafe
news.para: Following a fatal fire in Lucknow, Nagpur's alarming fire safety situation for coaching centres has come to light. Over a hundred buildings housing these classes have been declared unsafe, with many facing utility disconnections for non-compliance. Despite repeated warnings and enforcement, a significant number of institutes remain in violation, raising serious concerns for the thousands of students attending these facilities.

news.title: Sealing drive leaves young executives 'homeless' in Gurugram
news.para: Young professionals in Gurgaon's DLF-3 faced sudden displacement as authorities resumed a sealing drive against illegal constructions. Residents of paying guest accommodations and rented flats were given mere hours to pack and leave their homes, often discovering the crackdown upon returning from work. This action follows court orders targeting buildings violating approved plans and operating unauthorized PGs.

news.title: Over 46,000 unauthorised properties in Nashik being taxed with penalties: Maharashtra deputy CM
news.para: Nearly 46,389 properties in Nashik are under unauthorized occupation and facing penalties, the state legislature was informed. The Nashik Municipal Corporation is taxing these properties, many lacking essential occupancy certificates, under various Maharashtra Municipal Corporation Act provisions. Fines range from Rs 1,500 for residential units to Rs 30,000 for large establishments, with collected amounts going to the municipal treasury.

news.title: Manglam Group to invest ₹350 crore in Sheraton hotel on Jaipur-Ajmer highway
news.para: Manglam Group has signed an agreement with Marriott International to develop a 220-key Sheraton hotel in Jaipur. The project will be spread across more than three lakh sq ft and is part of the group’s ₹1,000 crore hospitality investment plan.
